company: a comedian and zoologist who has delighted audiences in over 20 countries, this show combines clever science tricks with laugh-out-loud moments, all using everyday objects kids can find at home. Each performance demystifies scientific concepts, encouraging children to see themselves as budding scientists who can experiment, explore, and discover on their own. Science Magic is more than entertainment; it’s an accessible gateway to curiosity and learning, priced affordably so that every child, regardless of background, can experience the wonder of science. Let’s inspire the next generation of scientists by showing them that magic is real—when you understand the science behind it!

We host regular monthly meetings, events, workshops, and campaigns to strengthen our industry, promote fair working conditions, and build a supportive network for local talent. title: Face It Till You Make It The Panic Years
company: Shouldn’t Have Said That Theatre Company. This bold, unfiltered show dives into the messy, hilarious, and painfully relatable reality of stumbling through your late twenties on the brink of thirty. Through sharp storytelling and brutally honest humour, it exposes the pressures of pretending to have life figured out while juggling careers, relationships, social lives, and the relentless expectations of social media. From the awkward intimacy of smear tests to the clash between generations, and the chaos of adulthood no one warned us about, this show celebrates imperfection and speaks the truths we all think but rarely dare to say out loud. A love letter and a reality check for anyone learning to survive adulthood one questionable decision at a time.

title: From Nightingales To Butterflies English Cello And Piano Music
company: nature, and the transformative healing power of family and legacy. Central to the programme are the cello sonatas of Frederick Delius and Frank Bridge (written, respectively, between 1916-1917 for the celebrated cellist, Beatrice Harrison, who was famed for her live recordings of nightingale and cello duets in the English countryside). The programme also features Stephanie Cant’s 'Suite in Ancient Style', composed in 1995 (for Esther) and based on a piece by her daughter, Kate Cooper (who loved butterflies!), and the premiere of Peter Copley’s ‘Nocturnal’, composed in 2021 for his daughter, Maria’s Copley 18th birthday. Esther and Evgenia are honoured to present this event in support of Off the Fence, and in dedication of Kate’s passionate wish to support the homeless.

title: Ai Mozart A 23Rd Century Concert
company: Dame Wendy Hall’s Web Science Institute, SELLOUT EdFringe25 pianist Shiel performs music composed by AI. In 2025, Matthew Shiel and Fringe Classics made history by performing the world’s first piano recital of music composed by artificial intelligence at the Edinburgh Festival and this historic event was broadcast on Al Jazeera. AI Mozart is a ground-breaking live concert of original piano pieces composed by artificial intelligences in an eclectic variety of styles from Beethoven and Brahms to boogie-woogie and Latin salsa to ridiculous hybrid genres including Viking progressive death polka! Step into the future of music and be the first to listen to an AI composer LIVE at the Brighton Fringe!
